title: The AI Canon: a method for auditable knowledge curation (Corpus Cognitivum)
description: Method note for The AI Canon, a public-good reference library of the texts that define artificial intelligence. The note specifies a method for auditable knowledge curation: rankings are produced by a published, deterministic procedure over declared evidence, so any reader can reproduce a result and challenge it. It states the frozen ontology (canonical entities are scored, context entities are described and never ranked, structurally), the deterministic and domain-isolated scoring, the handling of missing evidence (recorded and penalized, never imputed), the per-ecosystem normalization rule and its declared deferral, entity-resolution safety, the append-only governance and challenge model, and the reproducibility guarantee (every release rebuilds bit-for-bit from a self-contained audit package). It is honest about current coverage: the pilot scores the papers domain only, and the Chinese-language spine is a declared gap. Nothing is for sale, nothing is hidden, nothing is final.
notes: Companion code and corpus: The AI Canon pipeline and audit package. The method is versioned; subsequent versions cite the same concept DOI.
